title: phyllocladus trichomanoides var. trichomanoides
intro: Phyllocladus trichomanoides var. trichomanoides, also known as the white pine, is a variety of the Phyllocladus genus, which is a type of coniferous tree native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of Phyllocladus trichomanoides var. trichomanoides include its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Phyllocladus trichomanoides var. trichomanoides are typically made from the leaves, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that are used to treat a range of health issues.
